headline: CoreWeave (CRWV) Is Down 15.1% After Meta Weighs Selling Excess AI Compute Capacity – What's Changed

At the same time, investor anxiety has risen as Meta Platforms, a major CoreWeave customer, explores selling its own excess AI compute capacity, intensifying...

headline: AI Weekly: Anthropic controls lifted, Meta in the clouds
summary:
STORY: From Anthropic's new models getting cleared to Meta's reported big cloud move...

This is AI Weekly.

:: AI Weekly

Anthropic said the U.S. lifted export controls on its latest AI models.

It comes less than three weeks after the startup was forced to suspend access to its newest products over national security worries.

It led Anthropic to suddenly disable its Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models.

Washington has stepped up oversight of new releases due to concerns AI could be misused by intelligence agencies in China, Russia and elsewhere.

South Korea unveiled multi-billion dollar chip and AI mega-projects.

President Lee Jae Myung pledged investments worth more than $576 billion over a number of years.

Authorities said tech heavyweights Samsung and SK Hynix will invest over $518 billion to build two new chip fabrication sites each in South Korea's southwest.

It marks President Lee's biggest move yet to revive domestic economies beyond the capital Seoul.

Chipmaker Micron briefly blew past the market value of Big Tech giants Meta and Tesla for the first time ever.

The stock surge came a day after Micron forecast quarterly profit and revenue way ahead of analyst expectations.

The company is a major supplier to AI powerhouse Nvidia, and its shares have more than tripled in value so far this year.

It reached a market cap of about $1.4 trillion at one point after its results were released.

And Meta is building a cloud business to sell excess AI computing capacity.

That's according to a report from Bloomberg.

The move could lower Meta's reliance on advertising revenue and help it take on cloud majors like Amazon and Microsoft.

Meta didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

CEO Mark Zuckerberg said earlier this year the firm could enter the cloud computing business if it overspends on data centers and has excess capacity.

headline: AI Race Fuels New Fears of Crowded Trading
summary: Asian semiconductor stocks tumbled on Thursday, tracking tech losses on Wall Street after Meta Platforms Inc.'s plan to develop a business that would sell access to AI computing power raised worries about overcapacity. Brian Belski, CEO and founder at Humilis Investments, discusses the recent tech sector rout and what stocks he is keeping an eye on.

content.title: AI Race Fuels New Fears of Crowded Trading
content.description:
Asian semiconductor stocks tumbled on Thursday, tracking tech losses on Wall Street after Meta Platforms Inc.'s plan to develop a business that would sell access to AI computing power raised worries about overcapacity.
Brian Belski, CEO and founder at Humilis Investments, discusses the recent tech sector rout and what stocks he is keeping an eye on.

headline: Crusoe in talks to raise $3 billion funding, Bloomberg News reports
summary: AI data center startup Crusoe is in talks to raise about $3 billion in a funding ‌round that may triple its valuation, Bloomberg News ‌reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the situation.  The startup has contracts to ​supply AI computing power for the likes of Meta and Oracle, the report said, as technology giants spend billions of dollars on data centers to meet the massive computing requirements for ‌GenAI.  The startup is ⁠still in active talks on the round, and a final valuation has not been set, according to the report.

headline: Exclusive-Zuckerberg says AI agent tech is developing more slowly than expected
summary: Meta Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg told an internal town hall on Thursday that AI agent ‌development over the last four months had not "accelerated in the way we expected," ‌according to a recording heard by Reuters.  Zuckerberg added that a company reorganization that included major job cuts was ​not as "clean" as it could have been and that executives had miscalculated on the timing of the changes.  Conversations he was having "with our top people" when they started planning the restructuring in January and February "were that they were worried that we weren't going to move fast enough ‌to adapt," Zuckerberg said.

content.title: Exclusive-Zuckerberg says AI agent tech is developing more slowly than expected
content.summary: Meta Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg told an internal town hall on Thursday that AI agent ‌development over the last four months had not "accelerated in the way we expected," ‌according to a recording heard by Reuters.  Zuckerberg added that a company reorganization that included major job cuts was ​not as "clean" as it could have been and that executives had miscalculated on the timing of the changes.  Conversations he was having "with our top people" when they started planning the restructuring in January and February "were that they were worried that we weren't going to move fast enough ‌to adapt," Zuckerberg said.

headline: Exclusive-Meta's Zuckerberg says AI agent tech progressing slower than expected
summary: Meta Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged shortcomings in the company's sweeping restructuring at an internal town hall on Thursday, saying the systems known as AI agents ‌had not progressed as quickly as he had expected, according to a recording heard by Reuters.  Zuckerberg added that ‌a company reorganization that included major job cuts was not as "clean" as it could have been and that executives had miscalculated on the timing of the changes.  Zuckerberg ​and other Meta executives have been seeking to moderate some of the organizational changes introduced earlier this year, without fundamentally changing course.

content.title: Exclusive-Meta's Zuckerberg says AI agent tech progressing slower than expected
content.summary: Meta Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged shortcomings in the company's sweeping restructuring at an internal town hall on Thursday, saying the systems known as AI agents ‌had not progressed as quickly as he had expected, according to a recording heard by Reuters.  Zuckerberg added that ‌a company reorganization that included major job cuts was not as "clean" as it could have been and that executives had miscalculated on the timing of the changes.  Zuckerberg ​and other Meta executives have been seeking to moderate some of the organizational changes introduced earlier this year, without fundamentally changing course.
